data.title: China lifts curbs on export of rare earth minerals to the U.S.
data.description:
China has lifted its export restrictions on rare earth elements and other goods and technologies for military use, targeting 28 U.S. entities. The suspension of export controls took place on Wednesday and will last for 90 days, according to China’s Ministry of Commerce.

The move is a part of the broader agreement between the two countries to ease trade tensions, which also includes significant mutual tariff reductions.

China had imposed strict export bans on seven types of rare earths to 16 U.S. entities on April 4, later expanding the list to 28 entities. These restrictions were a countermeasure to U.S. tariff hikes.

Meanwhile, China will also remove a ban on trade and investment against 17 U.S. companies as part of the deal with the U.S.

China-related ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN).

U.S. market-tied ETFs: (SP500), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU)

data.title: Euro Area expects 1.2% Y/Y growth in Q1
data.description:
• GDP in the Euro Area expanded 0.30% in Q1 of 2025 over the previous quarter, below estimates of 0.4%.
  • GDP expanded 1.20% in Q1 2025 over the same quarter of the previous year, as expected.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: CSCO, BNGO, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped Thursday morning as investors weighed early developments in the US-China trade war, with attention now turning to upcoming inflation data and potential monetary policy signals from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s address.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) +4% — Shares rose after the company topped FQ3 estimates and issued strong guidance. Product orders jumped 20% Y/Y, with AI infrastructure orders exceeding $600M. For FQ4, Cisco guided revenue between $14.5B and $14.7B, with the midpoint above the $14.51B estimate, and projected adjusted EPS of $0.96–$0.98 vs. $0.95 consensus. It also raised its full-year revenue forecast to $56.5B–$56.7B (prior: $56B–$56.5B), topping the $56.4B consensus at the midpoint. FY non-GAAP EPS is now seen at $3.77–$3.79, in line with the $3.78 estimate.
  • Bionano Genomics (NASDAQ:BNGO) +20%— Shares jumped even as the company reported a 26% Y/Y revenue decline to $6.5M in Q1 2025. Despite the drop, Bionano improved gross margin to 46% from 32% a year earlier, driven by strategic cost cuts and product enhancements. Q1 operating expenses were slashed by 66% Y/Y. However, Bionano trimmed its 2025 revenue outlook to $26M–$30M from a prior range of $29M–$32M (vs. a consensus of $30.33M) and guided Q2 revenue to $6.3M–$6.8M, missing the $7.01M consensus. The company plans to install 15–20 new OGM systems this year.

Biggest stock losers

  • Luminar Technologies (NASDAQ:LAZR) -10% — Shares sank after the company reported mixed Q1 results and announced a leadership shake-up. Paul Ricci, former Nuance CEO, will take over as chief executive around May 21, 2025, following the resignation of founder Austin Russell as CEO and board chair due to a code of conduct inquiry by the board’s audit committee. The company emphasized that the issue does not impact financial results, and Russell will remain on the board. Luminar also said it expects to reduce quarterly non-GAAP OpEx by ~50% by the end of FY2025 compared to the start of FY2024.
  • New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) -31%— Shares plummeted after the company missed Q1 expectations, posting a GAAP EPS loss of -$0.73 (missed by $0.57) and revenue of $470.5M (down 31.8% Y/Y, missing by $144M). The sharp decline was driven by weak performance across segments. Looking ahead, NFE expects core earnings to improve with new developments in Brazil and Nicaragua and Puerto Rico expansions. The 624 MW CELBA plant in Brazil is ~95% complete and expected to contribute to earnings in Q3.

data.title: Avis Budget subsidiaries price $600M notes offering
data.description:
• Avis Budget Group (NASDAQ:CAR) subsidiaries, Avis Budget Car Rental and Avis Budget Finance, have priced a private offering of $600M of 8.375% senior notes due 2032.
  • The offering was upsized by $100M. The notes will have a maturity date of June 15, 2032, and will be issued at par.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, which may include repayment of indebtedness.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Belite Bio signals positive DSMB review and targets 500-patient PHOENIX trial enrollment completion in Q3 2025 while maintaining strong cash runway
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Belite Bio (BLTE) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Tom Lin reported continued progress in advancing Tinlarebant for Stargardt's disease and geographic atrophy, highlighting its status as a first-in-class oral therapy intended to reduce toxic vitamin A byproducts. Lin noted, "Tinlarebant has been granted Rare Pediatric Disease and Fast Track designations in the U.S. and Pioneer Drug designations in Japan. It has also been granted Orphan Drug designation in the U.S., Europe and Japan."
  • Lin announced, "As part of the Phase 3 DRAGON trial, we recently announced that the Data Safety Monitoring Board has completed its interim analysis... The DSMB recommended that the trial proceed without sample size increase, or modifications... In addition, they recommended that we submit the data for further regulatory review for drug approval."
  • The DRAGON 2 trial enrolled 16 of the targeted 60 subjects, including 10 Japanese subjects, with a focus on expediting a new drug application in Japan.
  • For the Phase 3 PHOENIX trial in geographic atrophy, Lin stated, "We expect PHOENIX trial to be fully enrolled for 500 subjects in Q3 this year."
  • CFO Hao-Yuan Chuang commented, "For Q1, 2025, we had R&D expenses of $9.4 million, compared to $6.8 million for the same period last year... G&A expenses, it was $6.1 million, compared to $1.6 million for the same period last year... Overall, we had a net loss of $14.3 million, compared to a net loss of $7.9 million for the same period last year." Chuang emphasized, "We had a cash increase of $12.3 million for the quarter, and leave us with $157.4 million in cash, liquidity funds, time deposit and U.S. treasury bills as end of Q1. We still expect four years of cash runway, and we expect to be able to complete all of our current clinical trials with this current cash."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated expectations to complete the DRAGON Phase 3 trial by the end of 2025 and to fully enroll the PHOENIX trial for 500 subjects in Q3 this year. Lin stated, "Completion of the trial is on track for end of this year." There was no explicit update on EPS or revenue guidance.
  • The company outlined plans to meet with regulators regarding Stargardt's disease and confirmed ongoing scheduling of meetings, but stated, "We don't have much to report back on" at this time.

Financial Results

  • R&D expenses were $9.4 million for Q1 2025, reflecting a year-over-year increase primarily due to share-based compensation and higher clinical trial expenses. G&A expenses rose to $6.1 million, also due to share-based compensation.
  • Net loss for the quarter was $14.3 million. Operating cash outflow was $8.3 million, with a cash increase of $12.3 million during the quarter, resulting in $157.4 million in total liquidity at quarter-end.
  • The company reported a strong cash position, with expectations to fund all current clinical trials with the existing cash runway.

Q&A

  • Basma Radwan, Leerink Partners, asked for an update on PHOENIX trial discontinuation rates and regulatory meetings for Stargardt's disease. Lin responded, "The dropout rate is approximately around about 20%... way below what's been reported in previous studies, such as the deuterated rate of vitamin A Phase 3 that was reported, I think, earlier this year, there's a dropout of about 30%." On regulatory meetings, Lin said, "We will be meeting a few of those in - we've scheduled some meetings in the short-term. We'll be updating that as we go along."
  • Jennifer Kim, Cantor, inquired about FDA interactions and regulatory risk. Lin said, "We haven't met up with the FDA yet. We'll be meeting up with that soon." Regarding regulatory risk, Lin stated, "I don't think that's going to affect us, especially... the division that's been giving us guidance throughout the whole study... most of them are still there. So I don't think there's any issues with the personnel change."
  • Kim also asked about efficacy and safety goalposts for Phase 3 data. Nathan Mata responded, "The study is powered to detect a 35% treatment effect between placebo and active... we're expecting that we'll be getting very close to our anticipated treatment effect size of 30% to 35%."
  • Yi Chen, HCW, asked about international pricing strategy and operating expenses. Lin stated, "We are still monitoring what's going on" regarding policy impacts. Chuang answered, "We expect it to be higher expenses given that most of the milestone of the studies, all three studies expect to be reached in the next one, two years, yes."
  • Bruce Jackson, Benchmark, asked about stock compensation and manufacturing location. Chuang explained, "For those that are going to be invested based on time, it will be allocated by the period of the duration of the option... I don't expect it's going to be that high moving forward this year." Lin added, "Tinlarebant is manufactured in the U.S. as well as in other geographies. So tariff is not going to affect us any way."
  • Michael Okunewitch, Maxim, inquired about PHOENIX interim analysis and sample size. Lin stated, "We're expecting to wrap up the enrollment, because that enrollment is going very smoothly as of now. We don't believe that we would need extra additional subjects."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about trial discontinuation rates, regulatory risk, goalposts for efficacy, operating expenses, and manufacturing strategy, with a generally neutral to slightly positive tone given the company’s progress and responses.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strong progress and financial position. Lin described the interim DSMB recommendation as "very optimistic and encouraged," and Chuang stressed the "strong cash position" and ability to fund trials.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management retained a stable, slightly positive tone, with management expressing optimism about clinical and operational milestones.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company advanced from enrolling over 400 to 464 subjects in the PHOENIX trial, now targeting full enrollment of 500 in Q3. The DRAGON 2 trial enrollment increased from 11 to 16 subjects.
  • Management reiterated the DSMB’s recommendation for regulatory review, consistent with the prior quarter.
  • Financially, R&D and G&A expenses rose significantly due to increased share-based compensation, while the net loss widened. The company’s cash position strengthened from the previous quarter.
  • Analyst focus shifted slightly more toward regulatory engagement and expense outlook, while management confidence in clinical progress and financial runway remained high. No significant change in sentiment was observed.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted a 20% dropout rate in the PHOENIX trial but emphasized this is lower than in comparable trials.
  • Regulatory uncertainties were raised, particularly regarding meetings with the FDA and global agencies, with management indicating ongoing discussions but no immediate concerns.
  • Questions were raised about future operating expenses, with management guiding for elevated costs in 2025 and 2026 due to clinical trial milestones before returning to lower levels.
  • Policy-related risks such as international drug pricing changes were acknowledged, with management monitoring developments but not anticipating immediate impacts.

Final Takeaway

Belite Bio highlighted notable clinical progress in Q1 2025, with the PHOENIX trial nearing its 500-subject enrollment target and the DRAGON trial maintaining momentum following a positive DSMB review. Management underscored the company’s strong cash position and projected ability to complete all ongoing trials, while addressing regulatory, operational, and market risks proactively. The quarter’s developments reinforce the company’s focus on advancing Tinlarebant as a first-in-class therapy for Stargardt’s disease and geographic atrophy, with key regulatory milestones and trial completions on track for later in the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: IEA cuts oil demand outlook as trade tensions, EV sales surge curb use
data.description:
The International Energy Agency (IEA) projects global oil demand growth to slow from 990,000 barrels per day ((bpd)) in the first quarter of 2025 to 650,000 bpd for the remainder of the year, as economic headwinds and record EV sales curb use.

Demand growth averages 740,000 bpd in 2025 and 760,000 bpd in 2026, despite accelerating OECD declines of -120 kb/d and -240 kb/d, respectively, the agency said.

Oil prices resumed their downward trajectory in late April and early May as trade tensions impacted financial and commodity markets and OPEC+ agreed to a further unwinding of production cuts.

Benchmark crude oil prices (CL1:COM) dropped by roughly $10/bbl through April and into May, driven by escalating U.S. tariffs and greater-than-anticipated increases in OPEC+ output. CL1:COM was down 3.5% to $60.91 at the time of writing.

Brent crude oil futures (CO1:COM) fell by $14/bbl in April to a four-year low of just above $60/bbl by early May. It was down 3.23% to $63.94 at the time of writing.

“Increased trade uncertainty is expected to weigh on the world economy and, by extension, oil demand,” the agency said.

With global supply projected to outpace demand growth significantly, oil inventories are expected to increase by an average of 720,000 barrels per day this year and 930,000 barrels per day in 2026, following a decline of 140,000 barrels per day in 2024. This shift signals a continued rebalancing of supply and demand fundamentals.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI)

data.title: Trump asks Apple to stop moving iPhone manufacturing to India - report
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump urged Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) CEO Tim Cook to stop building facilities in India, targeting the company's intention to diversify its manufacturing footprint beyond China, Bloomberg News report.

“I had a little problem with Tim Cook yesterday,” said Trump about his discussion with Cook in Qatar, part of his Middle East visit this week. “He is building all over India. I don’t want you building in India.” As a result of their conversation, Trump said Apple will be “upping their production in the United States,” the report added.

Trump noted that India has one of the highest tariff barriers in the world and it is difficult to sell U.S. goods in the country. Trump added that India has made an offer to reduce tariffs on U.S. goods, as the country eyes an agreement on import taxes, the report noted.

Apple and the White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Trump's comments could potentially impact Apple's plan, which aims to import most of its iPhones it sells in the U.S. from India by the end of next year, pivoting its manufacturing beyond China.

Apple still makes most of its iPhones in China and has no smartphone manufacturing in the U.S.

Apple's iPhone shipment contribution from India had increased in March ahead of Trump announcing a flurry of tariffs on April 2, according to Counterpoint Research.

Tariffs led to a shift in the country-of-origin mix away from China in the first quarter of 2025 with a strong uptick in shipments from India in March. India-made devices represented 26% of first quarter 2025 shipments, up from 16% in the year ago period. However, the majority of phones still came in from China, which had a 52% share in the first quarter of 2025 versus 56% in the same period a year ago, as per Counterpoint. 

On Wednesday, it was reported that iPhone assembler Foxconn (OTCPK:FXCOF) received approval from India to construct a $435M fabrication plant in the Asian nation.

A majority of India-made iPhones are assembled at Foxconn's factory in southern India. Tata Group’s electronics manufacturing unit, which acquired Wistron's local business and runs Pegatron's operations in India, is also a vital supplier of iPhones.

The iPhone's production in India rose 60% in the 12 months through March to $22B.

data.title: NetEase Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.44 beats by $0.52, revenue of $4B beats by $60M
data.description:
• NetEase press release (NASDAQ:NTES): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.44 beats by $0.52.
  • Revenue of $4B (+7.4% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Games and related value-added services net revenues were RMB24.0 billion (US$3.3 billion), an increase of 12.1% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • Youdao net revenues were RMB1.3 billion (US$178.9 million), a decrease of 6.7% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • NetEase Cloud Music net revenues were RMB1.9 billion (US$256.1 million), a decrease of 8.4% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • Innovative businesses and others net revenues were RMB1.6 billion (US$223.8 million), a decrease of 17.6% compared with the same quarter of 2024.

data.title: Youdao Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.09, revenue of $178.9M misses by $4.86M
data.description:
• Youdao press release (NYSE:DAO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.09.

  • "Looking ahead, we will accelerate the deployment of our large language model, Confucius, across learning and advertising verticals as part of our AI-native strategy — enhancing user experience while further boosting profitability. Having achieved our first full year of operating profit in 2024, we are well-positioned to sustain this momentum and deliver stronger profit growth and full-year operating cash flow breakeven in 2025," Dr. Zhou concluded.

data.title: Putin won't attend Russia-Ukraine talks in Turkey
data.description:
Russian President Vladimir Putin will not participate in talks with Ukraine in Turkey scheduled for Thursday, rejecting Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy's calls for him to attend.

Putin had called on Ukraine to "resume direct negotiations without any preconditions" on Thursday in Turkey, which U.S. President Donald Trump had backed. Zelenskyy replied that Ukraine was ready to negotiate.

"I will be in Turkey this Thursday, and I expect Putin to come as well. Personally. And I hope that this time, Putin won’t be looking for excuses as to why he 'can't' make it," Zelenskyy said at the time.

But the Kremlin on Wednesday revealed that the Russian delegation traveling to Turkey would not include Putin. It will be led by presidential aide Vladimir Medinsky, and will include Russia's deputy foreign minister, deputy defense minister, and the head of its military intelligence agency.

"Putin is the one who determines everything in Russia, so he is the one who has to resolve the war. This is his war. Therefore, the negotiations should be with him," Zelenskyy insisted. 

Trump—who's currently on a three-day Middle East trip—will not attend the talks in Turkey, after previously saying he might consider it "if it is appropriate." Instead, the talks with be attended by two U.S. envoys and Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
